intro.title: What group therapy offers that individual therapy doesn't
intro.text1: People seek out group therapy because it offers unique benefits that individual therapy alone can't provide: healing through connection. Where individual therapy focuses on one-on-one, personalized support, group therapy provides an interpersonal-based experience that can foster healing in different ways.
intro.quote: A space to feel seen, heard, and understood by people who get it.

benefits.title: Connection & Belonging
benefits.text: One of the biggest benefits of group therapy is realizing you’re not alone. Group therapy provides a space to connect with others who have similar experiences, which can be deeply validating and reduce feelings of shame or loneliness.

benefits.title: Peer Support & Feedback
benefits.text: Group members offer honest, compassionate feedback that can help you gain new perspectives. You’ll learn how others see you and how your behaviors impact those around you.

benefits.title: Opportunities for Practice - and for Healing
benefits.text: Many of our deepest wounds are relational. Group therapy allows you to heal in a relational setting. By building trust and connection with group members, you can start to shift unhealthy patterns and learn new ways of relating.

featured_group.title: Holding It Together
featured_group.subtitle: Group Therapy for Adults Who Are Quietly Struggling With Overwhelm or Disconnection
featured_group.text: You show up for work, for family, for friends. To the outside world, you look high functioning and capable. But inside, the emotional load can get overwhelming.
